envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: Microsoft 365 connector readiness substrate per ADR-0084. Spans Teams / Outlook / SharePoint / OneDrive via Microsoft Graph API.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Suggest-only. Activation requires Foundation governance + map-region approval + per-connector RULE 21 research arc completion.

items.MCP_posture: Microsoft MCP servers emerging (community + Microsoft-affiliated). Substrate-honest evaluation deferred to a later C-slice pre-flight. Initial path is direct Microsoft Graph API via OAuth 2.0 + Entra ID app registration.
items.OAuth_model: Microsoft Graph supports two permission models: delegated permissions (scopes acting on behalf of signed-in user) and application permissions (app roles for daemon scenarios without a user). OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for delegated; client credentials flow for application permissions.
items.app_installation_model: Entra ID (Azure AD) app registration per environment. App can be single-tenant or multi-tenant. Marketplace listing (AppSource) optional for distribution.
items.admin_consent_model: Tenant administrator pre-approval required for application permissions and for higher-privilege delegated scopes. Admin consent must be granted in the App Registration BEFORE users can be prompted for these scopes — dynamic-scope requests not pre-approved will fail.
read_capabilities: Team.ReadBasic.All / Channel.ReadBasic.All / ChannelMessage.Read.All (Teams reads — admin consent required)
User.Read / User.Read.All (directory reads — admin consent required for .All variants)
write_capabilities: Mail.Send (send mail as signed-in user — disabled by default)
Calendars.ReadWrite (calendar create / update — disabled by default)
Files.ReadWrite / Files.ReadWrite.All (OneDrive / SharePoint writes — disabled by default)
Sites.ReadWrite.All (SharePoint writes — disabled by default; admin consent)
Chat.ReadWrite / Channel.SendMessage (Teams write — disabled by default)
webhook_or_event_capabilities: Microsoft Graph change notifications (webhooks) via subscriptions API
Subscriptions for mail / calendar / Drive / Teams resources
Webhook validation handshake (client must respond to validation token at subscription create)
Renewal required (max subscription lifetime varies by resource)
risky_write_actions: User.ReadWrite.All / Directory writes (admin scope — irreversible identity ops)
required_approval_gates: Admin policy approval at activation per ADR-0084
Per-call governance approval for any write action
Entra ID admin consent for app permissions + .All-suffixed delegated scopes
dual_control_recommendations: SharePoint / OneDrive external sharing — dual-control recommended
Teams Channel.SendMessage to org-wide channels — dual-control
DMW_scope_implications: Per-user delegated reads scope to PERSONAL DMW per ADR-0046 (Personal AI Agent context)
Application-permission reads scope to ENTERPRISE DMW (Enterprise AI Agent context)
Sensitive sites / channels (HR / Legal / Compliance) excluded by Risk Map
workflow_purpose_bindings: ADR-0081 Stage 2: Outlook triage + summary recommendations
ADR-0081 Stage 2: Calendar conflict + preparation recommendations
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted Outlook replies awaiting user approval
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted Teams messages awaiting user approval
items.billing_pack_mapping: Workspace / Knowledge Pack + Collaboration Pack families per ADR-0083 Amendment 1 §9.4 (mapped to both connector-pack.workspace-knowledge.v1 + connector-pack.collaboration.v1 depending on sub-tool).
Dandelion_map_dependencies: Tool Map must approve Microsoft 365 (sub-tool list: Outlook / Teams / SharePoint / OneDrive)
Workflow Map must approve M365-bound workflows by purpose
Authority Map must approve send / share / write authority
Memory / DMW Map must approve M365 memory scope per surface
Risk Map must clear M365 (compliance-sensitive surfaces flagged)
audit_expectations: Webhook subscription lifecycle audited (create / renew / expire)
Application-permission impersonation (which user acted-on) surfaced in audit
secret_handling_requirements: Entra ID client_id / client_secret per tenant via secret_ref env-var-NAMEs per ADR-0024 + ADR-0019
Certificate-based auth preferred for application permissions (vs client_secret)
Per-user OAuth tokens encrypted at ConnectorBinding row
no_leak_rules: Mail body / Teams message content / SharePoint file content never traverses responses outside the wallet's scope
User principal names / object IDs never exposed cross-tenant
Site / drive / channel IDs never exposed cross-tenant
client_secret / certificate private key never logged
tenant_isolation_requirements: Per-tenant Entra ID tenant (Microsoft 365 is tenant-isolated)
Multi-tenant app pattern requires explicit per-customer admin consent flow
ConnectorBinding enforces wallet_id + entity scope on every API call
items.rate_limit_notes: Microsoft Graph applies per-app + per-tenant throttling (varies by service: Outlook ~10,000 req/10min per mailbox; SharePoint ~600 req/min per app; Teams varies). Exponential backoff + Retry-After header per Microsoft guidance.
testing_strategy: Fixture-based per-service read tests (Mail / Calendar / Files / SharePoint / Teams)
implementation_risks: Admin consent surface is the main onboarding friction — Founder pricing decision may want this pre-approved at install
Confused-deputy via application permissions: app acting without a user requires explicit per-call attribution
Dynamic scope requests not pre-approved in App Registration will fail silently — implementation must list scopes upfront
Teams compliance recording surface is sensitive — keep excluded until explicit governance review
SharePoint external sharing is a major data-egress risk — keep disabled until C-write slice
items.first_slice_recommendation: C5 Microsoft 365 read-first connector runtime — Outlook + Calendar + OneDrive metadata + SharePoint site reads via Microsoft Graph delegated OAuth. Teams reads deferred to C5.5 (admin-consent-heavy). NO writes at C5. After C2 / C3 / C4 + GOVSEC.6.
